question.question: How do I create a ServiceNow ticket for an OpenShift incident?
question.notes: ServiceNow is explicitly excluded. Must return OUT_OF_SCOPE, never hallucinate.

response.answer_markdown:
### Creating a ServiceNow Ticket for an OpenShift Incident

To create a ServiceNow ticket for an OpenShift incident, you should gather information for debugging by running the command `$ oc adm must-gather` [S5]. Then, create a compressed file from the must-gather directory by running the command `$ tar cvaf must-gather.tar.gz <must_gather_directory>` [S5]. After that, attach the installation directory used during the deployment, excluding the /auth subdirectory, to your support case on the Red Hat Customer Portal [S5]. Additionally, attach all other data gathered from this procedure to your support case [S5]. However, the provided evidence does not specify the exact steps to create a ServiceNow ticket.

### What this does not establish
The provided evidence does not establish the exact steps to create a ServiceNow ticket, as it only mentions attaching data to a support case on the Red Hat Customer Portal [S5]. It also does not provide information on how to access or use the ServiceNow platform.

### Sources
[S1] Installing an On-Premise Cluster with the Agent-based Installer (OCP 4.16) — OCP 4.16, pp. 20–21
[S2] Installing an On-Premise Cluster with the Agent-based Installer (OCP 4.14) — OCP 4.14, pp. 50–51
[S3] Installing an On-Premise Cluster with the Agent-based Installer (OCP 4.14) — OCP 4.14, pp. 27–28
[S4] Installing an On-Premise Cluster with the Agent-based Installer (OCP 4.14) — OCP 4.14, pp. 84–85
[S5] Installing an On-Premise Cluster with the Agent-based Installer (OCP 4.16) — OCP 4.16, pp. 61–62
[S6] Installing an On-Premise Cluster with the Agent-based Installer (OCP 4.16) — OCP 4.16, pp. 1–5
